name: Charlie Haden
abstract: Charles Edward Haden (August 6, 1937 – July 11, 2014) was an American jazz double bass player, bandleader, composer and educator whose career spanned more than fifty years. Haden helped to revolutionize the harmonic concept of bass playing in jazz, evolving a style that sometimes complemented the soloist, and other times moved independen…

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Watts
about_quotes.evidence: All the things I do with Charlie Haden, I’ve done like five albums with Charlie Haden with the Quartet West. Quartet projects. String projects. Big string projects where we worked with Shirley Horn and Bill Henderson
about_quotes.doc_title: Ernie Watts interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: Playing with guys like that, with Roy and Charlie Haden, made me take chances.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jones Hank
about_quotes.evidence: he was on the date Charlie Haden and I together to do that CD we did.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Broadbent
about_quotes.evidence: And I got off the road and got into Charlie Haden... they didn’t have a keyboard player, could I come down... So I went down to whatever gig it was... And we went up and there was the brass players and everything
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Broadbent interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 26, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Jane Ira Bloom
about_quotes.evidence: Playing with Charlie Haden and Ed Blackwell was probably the most exciting rhythm section I’ve ever worked with in my life, and I played the Village Vanguard with them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jane Ira Bloom interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 3, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Alvin Queen
about_quotes.evidence: And from that tour we had Charlie Haden opened up, we had Joe Henderson opened up, and the Kansas City Big Band was the most popular band of the evening.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alvin Queen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 23, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]
